description: Table states du mod TCP (colonnes documentees via le D2R Data Guide).
source: Colonnes du .txt (mod TCP) ; types/descriptions du D2R Data Guide (guide/).

columns.name: state
columns.description: Defines the unique name ID for the state

columns.name: group
columns.description: Assigns the state to a group ID value. This means that only 1 state with that group ID can be active at any time on a unit. If this value is empty, then ignore this

columns.name: remhit
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will be removed when the unit is hit. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: nosend
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state change will not be sent to the client. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: transform
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will be flagged to change the unit's appearance and reset its animations when it is applied. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: aura
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will be treated as an aura. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: curable
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state can be cured (This can be checked by NPC healing or the Paladin Cleansing skill). If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: curse
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will be flagged as a curse. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: active
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will be classified as an active state which enables the

columns.name: restrict
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will restrict the usage of certain skills (This connects with the

columns.name: disguise
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will be flagged as a disguise, meaning that the unit's appearance is changed, which can affect how the animations are treated when being used. If equals 0, then ignored this

columns.name: attblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make the related Attack Rating value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: damblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make related Damage value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: armblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Defense value (Armor) in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rfblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Fire Resistance value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rlblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Lightning Resistance value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rcblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Cold Resistance value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: stambarblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make the Stamina Bar UI in the HUD be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rpblue
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Poison Resistance value in the character screen be colored blue. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: attred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make the related Attack Rating value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: damred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make related Damage value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: armred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Defense value (Armor) in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rfred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Fire Resistance value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rlred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Lightning Resistance value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rcred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Cold Resistance value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: rpred
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make Poison Resistance value in the character screen be colored red. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: exp
columns.description: If equals 1, then a unit with this state will give exp when killed or will gain exp when killing another unit. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: plrstaydeath
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will persist on the player after that player is killed. If equals 0, then ignore this. state stays after death

columns.name: monstaydeath
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will persist on the monster (non-boss) after that monster is killed. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: bossstaydeath
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will persist on the boss after that boss is killed. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: hide
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will hide the unit when dead (corpse and death animations will not be drawn). If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: hidedead
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will be used to destroy units with invisible corpses. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: shatter
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state causes ice shatter missiles to create when the unit dies. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: udead
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state flags the unit as a used dead corpse and the unit cannot be targeted for corpse skills. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: life
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will cancel out the monster's normal life regeneration. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: green
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state overrides the color changes the unit and the unit will be colored green. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: pgsv
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state is flagged as part of a progressive skill which relates to charge-up skill functionalities. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: nooverlays
columns.description: If equals 1, then the standard way for States to add overlays will be disabled. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: noclear
columns.description: If equals 1, then when this state is applied on the unit, it will not clear stats that have this state from the state's previous application. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: bossinv
columns.description: If equals 1, then the unit with this state will use the state's source unit's (in this case, the unit's boss) inventory for generating the unit's equipped item graphics. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: meleeonly
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will make the all the unit's attack become melee attacks. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: notondead
columns.description: If equals 1, then the state will not play its On function (function that happens when the state is applied) if the unit is dead. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: pgsvoverlay
columns.description: Controls which overlay to use when the state has progressive charges on the unit, such as for the charge-up stat when using Assassin Martial Arts charge-up skills (Uses the

columns.name: castoverlay
columns.description: Controls which overlay to use when the state is initially applied on the unit (Uses the

columns.name: removerlay
columns.description: Controls which overlay to use when the state is removed from the unit (Uses the

columns.name: stat
columns.description: Controls the stat associated with the stat. This is also used when determining how to add the progressive overlay (Uses the

columns.name: setfunc
columns.description: Controls the client side set functions for when the state is initially applied on the unit

columns.name: remfunc
columns.description: Controls the client side remove functions for when the state is removed from the unit

columns.name: missile
columns.description: Used as a possible parameter for the

columns.name: skill
columns.description: Used as a possible parameter for the

columns.name: itemtype
columns.description: Defines a potential

columns.name: itemtrans
columns.description: Controls the color change of the item when the unit has this state. Referenced from the

columns.name: colorpri
columns.description: Defines the priority of the state's color change, when compared to other current sates on the unit. The current state that has the highest color priority on the unit will be used and other state colors will be ignored. If multiple current States share the same color priority value, then the game will choose the state with the lower ID value (based on where in the list of States in the data file that the state is defined)

columns.name: colorshift
columns.description: Controls which index of the color shift palette to use.

columns.name: light-r
columns.description: Controls the state's change of the red color value of the Light radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: light-g
columns.description: Controls the state's change of the green color value of the Light radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: light-b
columns.description: Controls the state's change of the blue color value of the Light radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: onsound
columns.description: Plays a sound when the state is initially applied to the unit. Links to a

columns.name: offsound
columns.description: Plays a sound when the state is removed from the unit. Links to a

columns.name: gfxtype
columns.description: Controls the how to handle the unit graphics transformation based on the unit type (This relies on the

columns.name: gfxclass
columns.description: Control's the unit class used for handling the unit graphics transformation. This field relies on what unit type was used in the

columns.name: cltevent
columns.description: Controls the event to check on the client side to determine when to use the function defined in the

columns.name: clteventfunc
columns.description: Controls the client Unit event function that is called when the event is determined in the

columns.name: cltactivefunc
columns.description: Controls the Client Do function that is called every frame while the state is active (the

columns.name: srvactivefunc
columns.description: Controls the Server Do function that is called every frame while the state is active (the

columns.name: canstack
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state can stack with duplicate forms of itself (This is only usable with the "poison" state). If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: sunderfull
columns.description: If equals 1, then this state will reapply any negative resistance stats at full potential when calculating pierce immunity if the immunity was broken. If equals 0, then reapply at the normal reduced efficiency (currently 1/5).
